request.prompt:
REVIEW-ONLY: validate this draft requirement set for a VS Code extension feature. Do NOT modify anything. Reply AGREE or list concrete MATERIAL objections (gaps, contradictions, unverifiable criteria, missing scope), each tagged critical, major, or suggestion, with the requirement section it concerns. Evidence rule: every critical/major objection must cite the requirement section plus a concrete failure scenario; otherwise it is a suggestion. Do NOT flag: style preferences, theoretical risks requiring unlikely preconditions, rephrasings, defense-in-depth, refactors out of scope, or anything outside gaps/contradictions/verifiability/scope.

Context: the extension currently ships an '@rewst' VS Code chat participant that streams Rewst's own backend AI (RoboRewsty, GraphQL subscription) into Copilot Chat, with a custom text-protocol tool loop (workspace/edit/web/command/graphql tools), server-side approval handling, conversation resume, apply-to-file buttons, and source citations. The user wants RoboRewsty contributed as a model in VS Code's chat model picker via the finalized LanguageModelChatProvider API so no GitHub/Copilot account is needed, with full feature parity, participant retired unless justified.

DRAFT REQUIREMENTS:
# Feature: RoboRewsty as a native chat model (remove Copilot account requirement)

## Summary

- Today, Rewst Buddy's AI chat is the `@rewst` chat participant inside the VS Code chat view. The chat view only functions when a language model is available, which in practice requires a GitHub Copilot sign-in/plan. Users without Copilot accounts cannot use RoboRewsty.
- Outcome: RoboRewsty appears as a selectable model in VS Code chat's model picker. Chatting with it requires no GitHub account and no Copilot plan. (The free Copilot Chat extension may remain installed as the chat surface — accepted by the user.)
- The `@rewst` chat participant is retired, unless a parity feature can only be delivered as a participant — any such retention must be justified and recorded in DECISIONS.md.
- Full feature parity with the current participant experience:
  - Local workspace read tools, edit tools (with visible diffs), web tools, command tool, and the session-authenticated GraphQL tool — all governed by the same existing `rewst-buddy.ai.*` settings.
  - Rewst-side action approvals (Approve once / Always allow) still work.
  - Resuming previous Rewst conversations still works.
  - Applying suggested code to a file still works.
  - Source citations still shown.
- Everything else untouched: template sync, sessions, server features, existing settings names and meanings.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `package.json` contributes `languageModelChatProviders` (vendor + displayName) and the extension registers a `LanguageModelChatProvider` exposing RoboRewsty as a model (org/session resolution preserved: prior behavior — single session auto-selects, multiple sessions prompt or expose per-org models; decided in Layer 1). Verify: `npm run test:unit` provider tests exit 0; manual: model appears in the picker.
2. Selecting RoboRewsty and sending a chat message streams the answer through the existing `askRewstAi` streaming path. Verify: unit test with mocked conversation stream; manual smoke in Extension Development Host.
3. Provider declares the `toolCalling` capability; chat-supplied tool declarations are translated into RoboRewsty's existing text tool protocol, and parsed tool requests from the model output are emitted as `LanguageModelToolCallPart`s so VS Code runs tools and feeds `LanguageModelToolResultPart`s back as the next turn. Verify: unit tests of the translation layer (declared tools → injected instructions; model output → tool-call parts; tool results → follow-up message).
4. Rewst-specific tools (GraphQL, web, command, plus workspace/edit capabilities not natively covered by the chat UI's built-in agent tools) remain available per the existing settings. Verify: unit tests + manual agent-mode session.
5. A Rewst-side approval pause surfaces to the user with approve-once / always-allow choices and the turn resumes correctly after approval, without the participant. Verify: unit tests of the approval mapping; manual.
6. Previous Rewst conversations can be listed and continued in chat. Verify: unit test of the resume path; manual.
7. The `@rewst` participant registration and its package.json contributions are removed (or retention justified in DECISIONS.md). Verify: grep of package.json contributions and activation code.
8. `engines.vscode` raised to the minimum version where extension-contributed models need no sign-in (^1.122.0). Verify: package.json + CHANGELOG note.
9. Existing `rewst-buddy.ai.*` settings keep their names and semantics; docs updated (docs/features.md, docs/reference.md, README features-glance bullet, CHANGELOG). Verify: diff review against docs conventions.
10. Full unit suite green: `npm run test:unit` exit 0.

## Architecture notes (internal; bound at Layer 1, not here)

- Map `provideLanguageModelChatResponse` onto the existing `ConversationClient`/`askRewstAi` subscription machinery.
- RoboRewsty is stateful server-side (conversationId); the provider API is a stateless message-array contract — a conversation-mapping layer is required (strategy decided in Layer 1).
- Approvals likely map to a registered LanguageModelTool with VS Code's confirmation UI, else a text-protocol fallback surfaced as a command.
- Conversation resume likely a command (`Resume Rewst AI Conversation`) since model providers have no slash commands.
- Research (corroborated, 2 independent hosts): LM Chat Provider API finalized in VS Code 1.104 (Aug 2025); Copilot sign-in/plan requirement for such models removed in VS Code 1.122 (May 2026); chat UI (Copilot Chat extension) is free and open source.

result.rawOutput:
MATERIAL OBJECTIONS

- **major — Summary / AC8:** The no-GitHub/no-Copilot outcome is not directly verified. Failure scenario: implementation is tested on a signed-in developer machine, `engines.vscode` is raised, and RoboRewsty appears in the picker, but a signed-out user on VS Code 1.122 still gets a GitHub/Copilot gate or no chat surface. Add an explicit signed-out, no-Copilot-plan manual acceptance check.

- **major — AC3 / AC4:** Tool ownership is underspecified. AC3 translates chat-supplied tools, while AC4 requires Rewst-specific GraphQL/web/command/workspace/edit tools to remain available, but it does not say whether those are registered VS Code `languageModelTools`, provider-internal text-protocol tools, or both. Failure scenario: RoboRewsty emits a `LanguageModelToolCallPart` for `rewst_graphql`, but that tool was never declared/registered/passed in `options.tools`, so VS Code cannot invoke it and the turn stalls.

- **major — Summary parity / Acceptance criteria:** “Applying suggested code to a file still works” and “Source citations still shown” are required in Summary but have no dedicated acceptance criteria. Failure scenario: streaming, tool calls, approvals, resume, and docs tests pass, but apply-to-file buttons and citation rendering are dropped during provider migration with no failing criterion.

- **major — AC6 / Architecture notes:** Conversation continuation is not verifiable across multiple turns after resume. The notes correctly identify the provider API as stateless and requiring a mapping layer, but AC6 only tests “the resume path.” Failure scenario: a previous Rewst conversation is listed and the first resumed response uses the right `conversationId`, but the next user message starts a new backend conversation because the provider has no required mapping/persistence behavior.

Sources checked: VS Code’s current Language Model Chat Provider guide and API reference confirm the provider registration, `toolCalling`, response parts, and tool-result contract; VS Code 1.104 release notes confirm API finalization, and 1.122 release notes confirm BYOK without GitHub sign-in.  
https://code.visualstudio.com/api/extension-guides/ai/language-model-chat-provider  
https://code.visualstudio.com/api/references/vscode-api  
https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_104  
https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_122
